name: builtin-bug-fix
description: Small bug-fixing benchmark. Each case asks UR to fix a known defect in a fresh worktree and verify with a test command.

cases.prompt: You are in a fresh TypeScript repo. Create src/findIndex.ts containing a function that returns the index of a target in an array. It currently returns -1 for the last element because the loop stops too early. Fix the off-by-one bug, add a test file src/findIndex.test.ts using your preferred runner, and run the tests. Finish with VERDICT: PASS if tests pass, otherwise VERDICT: FAIL.

expect.testCommand: cd src && (ls findIndex.ts findIndex.test.ts && echo present)

cases.prompt: You are in a fresh JavaScript repo. Create src/greet.js with a greet(name) function. It currently crashes when name is null/undefined because it calls name.toUpperCase(). Add a null guard so it returns 'Hello, stranger' for missing input. Add a test file and run it. Finish with VERDICT: PASS if tests pass.

expect.testCommand: cd src && (ls greet.js greet.test.js && echo present)

cases.prompt: You are in a fresh TypeScript repo. Create src/fetcher.ts with an async fetchData() function that forgets to await a Promise, returning the Promise object instead of the resolved value. Fix it, add a test using a mocked Promise, and run the tests. Finish with VERDICT: PASS if tests pass.

expect.testCommand: cd src && (ls fetcher.ts fetcher.test.ts && echo present)
